title: thapsia garganica subsp. garganica
intro: Thapsia garganica subsp. garganica, also known as the Greek thapsia, is a plant species in the carrot family native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Thapsia garganica subsp. garganica include its ability to treat digestive issues, relieve pain, and reduce inflammation, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
